2026-04-17T06:57:47.6774687Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T06:57:47.6775643Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T06:57:47.6776695Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T06:57:47.6777348Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T06:57:47.6777880Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T06:57:47.6778291Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T06:57:47.6778564Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-17T06:57:47.6779163Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T06:57:47.6779559Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-17T06:57:47.6781136Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T06:57:47.6781627Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-17T07:00:31.1566502Z   
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1567361Z   1. **Chat isolation key enforcement** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header) - Not documented in README
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1568966Z   2. **Metadata validation constraints** (16 keys max, 64-char keys, 512-char values) - Not documented in README
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1570280Z   3. **Malformed response ID validation** - Not documented in README
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1572028Z   4. **FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy** - A new logging/observability feature - mentioned in changelog but not in README
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1573776Z   5. **HttpClient instrumentation** for outbound trace correlation - In Core package changelog, not in README
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1574896Z   
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1575353Z   
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1576530Z   6. **Breaking changes** to error shapes - now returning 404 instead of 400 in certain scenarios, plus updated error type names
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1577805Z   
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1581380Z   The README has significant gaps: the Troubleshooting section only covers basic error codes without explaining the new chat isolation enforcement, Key Concepts doesn't mention the isolation header or metadata constraints, and the Logging section barely touches on the new observability features. The chat isolation behavior and metadata validation requirements are completely undocumented in the README despite being critical for users.
2026-04-17T07:00:31.1584381Z

forever)\\nwith TryEvict (which removes from the dictionary immediately after\\nfinalization). This fixes a memory leak where completed executions\\naccumulated indefinitely in the ResponseExecutionTracker.\\n\\nAfter eviction, all API calls (GET, DELETE, Cancel, SSE replay) for\\ncompleted responses fall through to the storage provider, which is the\\ncorrect source of truth for persisted data.\\n\\nKey changes:\\n- ResponseExecutionTracker: replace MarkCompleted with TryEvict\\n- SseReplayResult: catch BadRequestException from stream provider\\n- InMemoryResponsesProvider: throw BadRequestException (not IOE) for\\n  missing event streams — gives honest error for both 'never across all endpoints\\n\\nWhen a response is created with a chat isolation key (x-agent-chat-isolation-key),\\nall subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must provide the same key.\\nMismatch or missing key returns standard 404 (indistinguishable from not-found)\\nto prevent cross-chat information leakage. No enforcement when created without a key.\\n\\nEnforcement points:\\n- In-flight (tracker): orchestrator GetAsync/CancelAsync, handler SSE GET/DELETE\\n- Persisted (provider): InMemoryResponsesProvider Get/Delete/InputItems\\n\\nImplementation:\\n- ResponseExecution.ChatIsolationKey: stores creation-time key for in-flight checks\\n- EnforceInFlightChatIsolation(): shared helper in handler and orchestrator\\n- InMemoryResponsesProvider._chatIsolationKeys: ConcurrentDictionary for persisted checks\\n\\n17 new E2E protocol tests covering all endpoints (match/mismatch/missing),\\nin-flight background responses, and error body verification.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add HttpClient instrumentation for outbound trace correlation\\n\\nRegister AddHttpClientInstrumentation() for both tracing and metrics in\\nthe OTLP-only path (non-Azure Monitor). 'caresp_*' IDs to use\\n  IdGenerator.NewResponseId() to avoid B40 rejections\\n- Add 3 new test files: MalformedIdProtocolTests (7 tests),\\n  MetadataConstraintProtocolTests (8 tests),\\n  CreationIdValidationProtocolTests (3 tests)\\n\\nAll 1890 tests pass.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints\\n\\nWhen a response is created with a chat isolation key (x-agent-chat-isolation-key),\\nall subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must provide the same key.\\nMismatch or missing key returns standard 404 (indistinguishable from not-found)\\nto prevent cross-chat information leakage. No enforcement when created without a key.\\n\\nEnforcement points:\\n- In-flight (tracker): orchestrator GetAsync/CancelAsync, handler SSE GET/DELETE\\n- Persisted (provider): InMemoryResponsesProvider Get/Delete/InputItems\\n\\nImplementation:\\n- ResponseExecution.ChatIsolationKey: stores creation-time key for in-flight checks\\n- EnforceInFlightChatIsolation(): shared helper in handler and orchestrator\\n- InMemoryResponsesProvider._chatIsolationKeys: ConcurrentDictionary for persisted checks\\n\\n17 new E2E protocol tests covering all endpoints (match/mismatch/missing),\\nin-flight background responses, and error body verification.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add HttpClient instrumentation for outbound trace correlation\\n\\nRegister AddHttpClientInstrumentation() for both tracing and metrics in\\nthe OTLP-only path (non-Azure Monitor). This exports outbound HTTP client\\nspans so distributed traces show the full call chain through the Foundry\\nstorage layer. UseAzureMonitor already registers both instrumentations,\\nso the existing guard prevents duplicate spans.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Update changelogs for Responses beta.2 and Core beta.22\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add structured logging to FoundryStorageProvider\\n\\nUse LoggerMessage source-generated methods to log every outbound Foundry\\nstorage API call with operation name, response ID, HTTP status code, and\\nduration. Debug level for success, Warning for HTTP errors, Error for\\nexceptions. Never logs credentials or PII.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Replace inline logging with FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy\\n\\nMove Foundry storage request/response logging from FoundryStorageProvider\\ninto a dedicated HttpPipeline per-retry policy. This captures correlation\\nheaders on both sides:\\n- Request: x-ms-client-request-id (stamped by Azure.Core)\\n- Response: x-ms-request-id (returned by Foundry service)\\n\\nEach retry attempt is logged independently with method, URI, status code,\\nand duration. FoundryStorageProvider reverts to its clean form with no\\nlogging dependency.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Log x-request-id and
